title: Sessions Are Braids: Measuring Latent Thread Structure in Real Coding-Agent Sessions
description: A measurement note on real coding-agent sessions: what the context window is actually made of by turn intent and by latent topic structure; evidence that 93% of sessions interleave topically distinct threads with a large cross-thread context share; and a component analysis of what condensation must preserve when context is scoped to a thread. Includes measurement scripts and aggregate results. Single-user corpus; feasibility signal, not a population estimate.
